source_paper_title: Dynamic Portfolio Selection for Nonlinear Law-Dependent Preferences
context:
Section 6 (Weighted utility), Remark 6.2(a), page 15 (arXiv v2 pagination)

It is worth noting that (6.4) is a two-dimensional QBSDE, whose two components \(\bar Y_1\) and \(\bar Y_2\) are coupled. Two-dimensional QBSDEs are technically much more challenging than the one-dimensional ones, and unless other assumptions are imposed, the well-posedness remains open; see the bibliographical notes in Subsection 7.4 of Zhang (2017).
title: Well-posedness of coupled multi-dimensional quadratic BSDEs arising in equilibrium portfolio selection with weighted utility
background_latex: Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb F,\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a one-dimensional Brownian motion \((W_t)_{0\le t\le T}\). Let \(\kappa:[0,T]\times\Omega\to\mathbb R\) be a bounded \(\mathbb F\)-predictable process, and define \(\Theta:=\int_0^T |\kappa(s)|^2\,ds\). Consider unknown adapted processes \(\bar Y_1,\bar Y_2\in L^\infty(\mathbb F)\) and \(\bar Z_1,\bar Z_2\) such that \(\bar Z_i\in H_{\mathrm{BMO}}\) (i.e., \(\int_0^T |\bar Z_i(s)|^2ds\) has bounded mean oscillation). Let \(\bar Z:=(\bar Z_1,\bar Z_2)^\dagger\in\mathbb R^2\). Fix constants \(C_1,C_2\in\mathbb R^{2\times 2}\), \(c_{i,j}\in\mathbb R\) for \(i,j\in\{1,2\}\), and \(b_1,b_2\in\mathbb R\). For \(i\in\{1,2\}\), write \(-i\) for the other index in \(\{1,2\}\).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove (or disprove) that the coupled two-dimensional quadratic BSDE system
\[
d\bar Y_i(s) = -\frac12\Big( \bar Z(s)^\dagger C_i\,\bar Z(s) + c_{i,i}\,\bar Z_i(s)\,\kappa(s) + c_{-i,i}\,\bar Z_{-i}(s)\,\kappa(s) + b_i\,|\kappa(s)|^2 \Big)\,ds + \bar Z_i(s)\,dW_s,
\quad s\in[0,T],\quad i\in\{1,2\},
\bar Y_i(T)=0,\quad i\in\{1,2\}.
\]
admits a unique adapted solution \(((\bar Y_1,\bar Y_2),(\bar Z_1,\bar Z_2))\) in \((L^\infty(\mathbb F))^2\times(H_{\mathrm{BMO}})^2\) under general conditions on \((C_i,c_{i,j},b_i,\kappa)\), without relying on special structural assumptions on the generator (e.g., linear-quadratic structure) or smallness assumptions (e.g., small \(V(\Theta):=\sup_{\tau}\|\Theta-\mathbb E_\tau[\Theta]\|_\infty\)).
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb F,\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a one-dimensional Brownian motion \((W_t)_{0\le t\le T}\). Let \(\kappa:[0,T]\times\Omega\to\mathbb R\) be a bounded \(\mathbb F\)-predictable process, and define \(\Theta:=\int_0^T |\kappa(s)|^2\,ds\). Consider unknown adapted processes \(\bar Y_1,\bar Y_2\in L^\infty(\mathbb F)\) and \(\bar Z_1,\bar Z_2\) such that \(\bar Z_i\in H_{\mathrm{BMO}}\) (i.e., \(\int_0^T |\bar Z_i(s)|^2ds\) has bounded mean oscillation). Let \(\bar Z:=(\bar Z_1,\bar Z_2)^\dagger\in\mathbb R^2\). Fix constants \(C_1,C_2\in\mathbb R^{2\times 2}\), \(c_{i,j}\in\mathbb R\) for \(i,j\in\{1,2\}\), and \(b_1,b_2\in\mathbb R\). For \(i\in\{1,2\}\), write \(-i\) for the other index in \(\{1,2\}\).

Prove (or disprove) that the coupled two-dimensional quadratic BSDE system
\[
d\bar Y_i(s) = -\frac12\Big( \bar Z(s)^\dagger C_i\,\bar Z(s) + c_{i,i}\,\bar Z_i(s)\,\kappa(s) + c_{-i,i}\,\bar Z_{-i}(s)\,\kappa(s) + b_i\,|\kappa(s)|^2 \Big)\,ds + \bar Z_i(s)\,dW_s,
\quad s\in[0,T],\quad i\in\{1,2\},
\bar Y_i(T)=0,\quad i\in\{1,2\}.
\]
admits a unique adapted solution \(((\bar Y_1,\bar Y_2),(\bar Z_1,\bar Z_2))\) in \((L^\infty(\mathbb F))^2\times(H_{\mathrm{BMO}})^2\) under general conditions on \((C_i,c_{i,j},b_i,\kappa)\), without relying on special structural assumptions on the generator (e.g., linear-quadratic structure) or smallness assumptions (e.g., small \(V(\Theta):=\sup_{\tau}\|\Theta-\mathbb E_\tau[\Theta]\|_\infty\)).
